column3: A group or body (formal or informal) acting collectively in a research, institutional, or professional context.

This model covers the full range of CRM actor groups: formally constituted legal bodies (E40), informal working groups and initiatives, and funding bodies. Organisations are identified with PIDs and system labels, described with text, typed by sector and legal status, linked to member persons, and associated with a place via the location model.
A person who is or was a member of the organisation.
The place associated with the organisation (e.g., headquarters, premises).
Documents or records relating to the organisation (e.g., founding charters, reports, authority records).
E41: Unique System Label or ID

column1: E41: Unique System Label or ID
column3: Required within some documentation or database systems, such as ResearchSpace required label.
The organisation's full preferred name as an appellation.
Short form or acronym (e.g., NG, ICOM, E-RIHS).
Any other name the organisation is known by, including former names.
Additional identifiers from authority sources (e.g., VIAF, ISNI, Wikidata, ROR, LEI).
A globally resolvable identifier for the organisation (e.g., Wikidata QID, ROR ID).
Sector or category of organisation, use controlled terms e.g.: museum, university, funding-body, research-group, working-group, initiative, company, government-body.
Formal legal standing of the organisation, use controlled terms e.g.: legal-body, registered-charity, informal-group, intergovernmental-organisation.
Contact information for the organisation; email address or telephone number.
A logo or other visual representation of the organisation.
A prescribed citation string for referencing the organisation or project in publications (e.g., for funded projects with a required acknowledgement form).
Free-text description of the organisation's purpose, scope, or history.
Documents relating to the organisation (authority files, reports, charters).
